floor_history.headline: fable-blitz recon: whole-body asmfix stub (asmfix.txt:89 replace_with_asmfile) - NOT a plateau; ~380-insn thrown-weapon flight-physics loop over the 12x0x64 D_80106A78 table, fully conventional GCC C

frontier.hypothesis: Full-body transcription from tmp/blitz/m2c_80030D7C.c using the sibling byte-offset idiom ((u8*)&D_80106A78 walkers, raw 0x1F8002B8 scratch pointers) lands a first honest floor far below 707, likely in the low tens - the function is conventional throughout.
frontier.mechanism: baseline probe - no wall engaged yet; every region uses documented-natural GCC 2.7.2 shapes (bias diamonds, sin-table indexing, mult/mflo interleave)
frontier.next_probe: write the body into src/code6cac_b.c:2682, `& tools/wteng.ps1 main sandbox func_80030D7C --disable all` for the real floor, then region-scope the diff

frontier.hypothesis: The R5 three-call funnel (asm 552-560) reproduces from m2c's nested if/else with three separate func_80032854 calls whose arg code suffixes are identical - jump2 cross-jumps them without any coaxing; if not, the cross-jump-call-merge levers (arg-count/arg-order) close it.
frontier.mechanism: jump2 find_cross_jump on identical CALL-suffix blocks (the sltu a0 + a3=0 + jal tail)
frontier.next_probe: after baseline, diff scoped to asm lines 436-560; verify labels .L80031590/9C/A0 shape (one call, three entries) vs three separate jals

frontier.hypothesis: The endgame plateau will be s-register assignment among the 9 callee-saved values + the sp+0x18 spill of 0x1F8002C8; declaration order and per-region ref counts (NOT pins) steer global.c to the target permutation, with the spilled constant emerging naturally as the lowest-ref value.
frontier.mechanism: global.c ref-count/priority allocation across s0-s7+fp; spill of the 10th-priority live range
frontier.next_probe: if renames persist after regions match structurally, -da greg dump on the sandbox .i and compare pseudo dispositions per register-alloc-pure-c step 0
